reason: The paper explicitly states "variable importance was estimated using the impurity method" and Table S1's importance ranking is used to name "the most important predictor" (an Rz-like lysis protein) and identify the "50 most important proteins," which is then used to support the central claim that "RaFAH learned to predict virus-host associations based on proteins that are directly involved in virus-host molecular interactions." This ranking-based mechanistic claim is corroborated only by qualitative biological plausibility (these protein types are known to be involved in host range) and by held-out validation of overall model accuracy (F1/precision/recall on Test Set 2), not by permutation importance, SHAP, PDP/ALE, conditional importance, or held-out predictive validation restricted to the selected top-feature subset. Since impurity importance is used, its magnitude/ranking is central to a stated biological conclusion, and no adequate corroboration of the ranking itself exists, all three conditions for CONFIRMED are met.
